content.text: I'll conduct a thorough audit of the "Project Intelligence & AI" module. Let me first research the competitive landscape to understand what best-in-class looks like.

input.query: ALICE Technologies AI construction schedule optimization

content.text:
Now I have comprehensive competitive intelligence. Let me conduct the deep audit of SiteSyncAI's "Project Intelligence & AI" module against all 14 dimensions.

Based on my analysis of the codebase, the competitive landscape, and the product vision, here is the complete audit:

```json
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 32,
    "domain_depth": 28,
    "data_richness": 22,
    "interaction_quality": 25,
    "ai_integration": 35,
    "mobile_first": 20,
    "performance": 38,
    "typescript_quality": 30,
    "error_handling": 40,
    "realtime": 35,
    "accessibility": 22,
    "security": 30,
    "differentiation": 25,
    "enterprise_readiness": 28
  },
  "overall_score": 29,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "ai_integration",
      "title": "AI Copilot has no real prediction engine — the north star 'Monday morning what goes wrong Thursday' feature is missing entirely",
      "description": "The product vision's #1 north star feature is 'AI that catches problems before they become delays. It reads the schedule, the weather, the RFI log, the delivery schedule, and tells you on Monday morning what is going to go wrong on Thursday.' The current codebase has src/lib/predictions.ts and src/components/ai/PredictiveAlert.tsx but the AI insights endpoint (src/api/endpoints/ai.ts) returns static rows from a database table with no actual prediction logic. getAiInsights just queries ai_insights table with dismissed=false. There is no engine that correlates schedule_phases + weather API + RFI due dates + crew productivity to generate forward-looking risk predictions. Procore now has Helix-powered risk agents that spot potential risks early and Autodesk has Construction IQ that flags safety and quality risks from real project data. ALICE Technologies simulates millions of schedule scenarios. SiteSync has none of this — the single most important differentiator is a database read of pre-seeded rows.",
      "prompt": "You are building the predictive intelligence engine for SiteSyncAI, a construction project management platform. This is the #1 differentiating feature — the ability to tell a PM on Monday morning what will go wrong by Thursday.

Files to create/modify:
1. Create `src/lib/predictiveEngine.ts` — the core prediction engine
2. Create `src/hooks/usePredictiveInsights.ts` — React hook for consuming predictions
3. Modify `src/api/endpoints/ai.ts` — add real-time prediction generation
4. Create `src/components/ai/PredictiveInsightCard.tsx` — visual card for each prediction
5. Modify `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` — integrate prediction cards into the main dashboard

Implementation for `src/lib/predictiveEngine.ts`:
- Export a `PredictionEngine` class with method `generatePredictions(projectData: ProjectContext): Promise<Prediction[]>`
- ProjectContext type includes: { schedulePhases: SchedulePhase[], rfis: RFI[], submittals: Submittal[], weather: WeatherForecast[], crews: Crew[], budgetItems: BudgetItem[], changeOrders: ChangeOrder[], dailyLogs: DailyLog[] }
- Prediction type: { id: string, type: 'schedule_risk' | 'weather_impact' | 'rfi_bottleneck' | 'budget_overrun' | 'resource_conflict' | 'submittal_delay' | 'safety_risk', severity: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low', title: string, description: string, affectedItems: { type: string, id: string, name: string }[], predictedDate: string, confidence: number (0-100), recommendedActions: string[], impactDays: number, impactCost: number, dataPoints: { label: string, value: string }[] }
- Implement these prediction algorithms:
  a) SCHEDULE_WEATHER_CONFLICT: Cross-reference schedule_phases where is_critical_path=true and have outdoor work tags (concrete, earthwork, roofing, exterior) with 5-day weather forecast. If rain >60% probability or temp <32°F or wind >25mph on a day when outdoor critical path activity is scheduled, generate a prediction. Calculate downstream impact using critical path dependencies.
  b) RFI_BOTTLENECK: Find RFIs where status='open' or 'under_review' and (due_date - today) < 3 days. Cross-reference with schedule activities that depend on the RFI response. Calculate days of potential delay if RFI is not answered by due date.
  c) SUBMITTAL_PROCUREMENT_RISK: Find submittals with status='in_review' or 'submitted' where the lead_time + today > required_on_site_date from schedule. Flag as 'material will arrive late even if approved today'.
  d) BUDGET_TREND_ANALYSIS: For each CSI division, calculate burn rate (actual_amount / percent_complete). If burn rate extrapolated to 100% exceeds (original_amount + approved_change_orders) by >5%, flag as budget overrun risk. Include projected overrun amount.
  e) RESOURCE_CONFLICT: Find schedule activities overlapping in the same week that require the same trade (from crews table). If total crew demand exceeds available crew size, flag as resource conflict.
  f) PRODUCTIVITY_DECLINE: Analyze last 5 daily_logs for each crew. If workers_onsite is declining or total_hours/workers ratio is dropping, flag as productivity concern with trend data.
  g) CASCADING_DELAY: If any critical path activity is behind schedule (percent_complete < expected based on elapsed time), calculate the cascade effect on all successor activities. Show the new projected completion date.
- Sort predictions by severity (critical first), then by predictedDate (soonest first).
- Each prediction must include specific, actionable recommendedActions written in construction superintendent language (e.g., 'Reschedule the Level 3 slab pour from Thursday to Monday. Notify Ready Mix dispatch by end of day Tuesday. Alert formwork crew lead about the schedule change.')

For `src/hooks/usePredictiveInsights.ts`:
- Use React Query to fetch all required project data in parallel
- Pass aggregated data to PredictionEngine.generatePredictions()
- Return { predictions, isLoading, criticalCount, highCount, nextRiskDate }
- Cache predictions for 15 minutes, auto-refresh
- Include a `dismissPrediction(id)` mutation that marks it as reviewed

For `src/components/ai/PredictiveInsightCard.tsx`:
- Design per the design standards: white card, 12px border-radius, 1px border #E5E7EB
- Left border: 4px colored by severity (red=critical, amber=high, blue=medium, gray=low)
- Header: severity badge (8px dot + text), prediction type icon (from Lucide: CloudRain for weather, Clock for schedule, DollarSign for budget, Users for resource, FileText for RFI, Package for submittal), confidence percentage as a small pill
- Title: 14px 600 weight
- Description: 14px 400 weight, muted color, max 3 lines
- Affected items: clickable chips that link to the actual RFI/submittal/schedule item
- Data points: key-value pairs in a subtle grid (12px muted labels, 14px values)
- Recommended actions: numbered list with checkboxes to mark as 'action taken'
- Impact summary: 'Potential delay: 3 days | Estimated cost impact: $45,000'
- Footer: 'Predicted for Thursday, April 3' with relative time, dismiss button, and 'Take Action' primary button
- Animate in with fade + translateY(8px) to translateY(0) in 150ms

For Dashboard integration:
- Add a 'Risk Forecast' section above the activity feed on the Dashboard
- Show top 3 predictions with a 'View all N risks' link
- If no risks: show a green success state: 'No risks detected for the next 5 days. Project is on track.'
- Include a sparkline showing risk count trend over the last 30 days

Acceptance criteria:
- At least 7 distinct prediction types are implemented with real calculation logic
- Every prediction includes specific affected items linked to real project entities
- Every prediction includes at least 2 actionable recommendations in plain construction English
- Predictions update automatically when underlying data changes
- A superintendent on their phone at 6am can see the top 3 risks for the week in under 2 seconds
- Cards are tappable on mobile with 44px minimum touch targets
- Works fully offline by using cached project data for predictions"
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "ai_integration",
      "title": "AI Copilot chat lacks construction domain knowledge and project context injection",
      "description": "The AI Copilot page (src/pages/AICopilot.tsx) and hook (src/hooks/useProjectAI.ts) exist but the conversation endpoint (src/api/endpoints/ai.ts getAiConversation) returns an empty array with a comment 'AI conversations are handled by useProjectAI hook'. The copilot must know all project data (schedule, budget, RFIs, submittals, daily logs, change orders), construction terminology, and proactively surface insights. Procore Assist now provides contextually relevant answers from specs, RFIs, submittals, and building codes in seconds. Procore also has Agent Builder letting users build custom AI agents without coding. SiteSync's copilot has no system prompt with construction domain knowledge, no project context injection, no tool-calling for real data lookups, and no proactive suggestion engine. The spec says the AI should be 'the smartest project engineer on the team' — right now it's a stub.",
      "prompt": "You are building the AI Copilot for SiteSyncAI — it must feel like having the smartest project engineer on your team, always available, never wrong about the facts.

Files to create/modify:
1. Create `src/lib/aiCopilot/systemPrompt.ts` — construction domain system prompt
2. Create `src/lib/aiCopilot/projectContextBuilder.ts` — assembles current project state for AI context
3. Create `src/lib/aiCopilot/toolDefinitions.ts` — define callable tools the AI can use to look up real project data
4. Create `src/lib/aiCopilot/responseFormatter.ts` — formats AI responses with linked entities
5. Modify `src/hooks/useProjectAI.ts` — wire up real AI conversation with streaming
6. Modify `src/components/ai/AIContextPanel.tsx` — upgrade the slide-in chat panel
7. Create `src/components/ai/SuggestedActions.tsx` — context-aware suggested prompts

For `src/lib/aiCopilot/systemPrompt.ts`:
- Export `buildSystemPrompt(projectContext: ProjectSummary): string`
- The system prompt must include:
  a) Role: 'You are an expert construction project engineer embedded in SiteSyncAI. You have deep knowledge of commercial construction, AIA billing, CPM scheduling, CSI MasterFormat, OSHA safety standards, and construction law.'
  b) Project context section: project name, contract value, start/end dates, current completion %, GC name, key team members
  c) Current status snapshot: open RFIs count, overdue submittals, budget variance by division, schedule variance, active crews, recent daily log highlights
  d) Behavioral rules: Always reference specific RFI numbers, submittal numbers, or cost codes. Never give generic advice. If you don't have data to answer, say exactly what data is missing. When discussing schedule, reference actual activity names and dates. When discussing money, give exact dollar amounts. Use construction industry terminology correctly (e.g., 'ball in court' not 'assigned to', 'schedule of values' not 'budget lines').
  e) Response format rules: Use markdown for formatting. Link to specific entities using a custom syntax: [[RFI:id|RFI-001]], [[SUBMITTAL:id|SUB-003]], [[CO:id|CO-005]]. These will be parsed into clickable links by the response formatter.
  f) Safety rules: Never fabricate data. Never give legal advice. Always recommend consulting with the project attorney for legal questions and the safety officer for safety incidents.

For `src/lib/aiCopilot/projectContextBuilder.ts`:
- Export `buildProjectContext(projectId: string): Promise<ProjectSummary>`
- Fetch in parallel: project details, all open RFIs (count + list of overdue ones), all pending submittals (with ball-in-court), budget summary by CSI division (original, committed, spent, variance), schedule phases (with % complete and critical path items), last 3 daily logs (weather, crew counts, incidents), all pending change orders (count and total pending value), active crews with current tasks
- Compress into a structured summary that fits within ~4000 tokens
- Include a 'hot items' section: top 5 most urgent things (overdue RFIs, submittals past due, budget items >10% over, critical path activities behind schedule)
- Cache this context for 5 minutes, invalidate on any data mutation

For `src/lib/aiCopilot/toolDefinitions.ts`:
- Define tools the AI can call to get fresh data:
  a) `lookupRFI(rfiNumber: string)` — returns full RFI detail including thread history
  b) `lookupSubmittal(submittalNumber: string)` — returns full submittal with revision history
  c) `queryBudget(division?: string)` — returns budget data, optionally filtered by CSI division
  d) `querySchedule(activityName?: string)` — returns schedule data with float and critical path info
  e) `searchDailyLogs(dateRange: { from: string, to: string }, keyword?: string)` — full-text search across daily logs
  f) `getWeatherForecast()` — returns 5-day weather for the project location
  g) `calculateCashFlow(weeks: number)` — generates cash flow projection
  h) `findRelatedItems(entityType: string, entityId: string)` — finds RFIs linked to a submittal, COs linked to an RFI, etc.
- Each tool returns typed data that gets injected back into the conversation

For `src/lib/aiCopilot/responseFormatter.ts`:
- Parse AI response markdown for entity links like [[RFI:uuid|RFI-001]]
- Convert to React components: clickable chips that navigate to the entity detail page
- Parse currency values and wrap in formatted spans
- Parse dates and show relative time tooltips
- Detect and render inline data tables from markdown tables
- Detect code blocks and render them appropriately

For `src/components/ai/SuggestedActions.tsx`:
- Generate context-aware suggested prompts based on current page:
  - On Dashboard: 'What are the top 3 risks this week?', 'Summarize yesterday's daily log', 'Which RFIs are blocking the schedule?'
  - On RFIs page: 'Draft a response for RFI-XXX', 'Which RFIs have cost impact?', 'Show me overdue RFIs by trade'
  - On Budget page: 'Which divisions are trending over budget?', 'What is the projected final cost?', 'Generate a cash flow forecast for the next 13 weeks'
  - On Schedule page: 'What is the current critical path?', 'How much float does electrical rough-in have?', 'Will we hit the substantial completion date?'
- Show as horizontally scrollable pill buttons below the chat input
- Max 5 suggestions per context, updated when page changes
- Each pill: 12px text, gray border, 8px border-radius, 32px height, tappable

For the upgraded AIContextPanel:
- Slide-in from right, 400px wide on desktop, full-screen on mobile
- Header: 'SiteSync AI' with a brain/sparkle icon, project name subtitle, close button
- Message list: scrollable, auto-scroll to bottom on new messages
- User messages: right-aligned, orange background, white text, 14px, 8px border-radius
- AI messages: left-aligned, white background, 1px border, 14px, 8px border-radius, with avatar
- Tool call indicators: subtle inline badges showing 'Looking up RFI-001...' with a spinning icon while tools execute
- Streaming: show AI response character-by-character with a blinking cursor
- Input: 40px height text area that expands to max 120px, send button (orange), attach file button, voice input button
- Suggested actions row above input
- Keyboard: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline, Cmd+K opens from anywhere
- Persist conversation in localStorage, clear button in header

Acceptance criteria:
- AI can answer 'What is the status of RFI-001?' and return the actual status, who has the ball, when it's due, and whether it has cost impact — all from real project data
- AI can answer 'Which divisions are over budget?' and return a formatted table of CSI divisions with variance amounts
- AI can draft an RFI response that references the project specs and similar past RFIs
- Suggested prompts change based on which page the user is currently viewing
- Entity links in AI responses are clickable and navigate to the correct detail page
- Conversation persists across page navigations and browser refreshes
- Response streaming renders smoothly at 60fps with no layout shifts
- Works on mobile with proper touch input and keyboard handling"
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "AI health score is a hardcoded placeholder (85) — no real calculation engine",
      "description": "In src/api/endpoints/projects.ts, the getMetrics function returns `aiHealthScore: 85` and `aiConfidenceLevel: 74` as hardcoded values. The product vision demands 'a project health score: composite metric from schedule variance, budget variance, safety rate, RFI turnaround.' The ProjectHealth page exists but if the underlying metrics are fake, nothing downstream is trustworthy. A CFO would never present hardcoded numbers to a board. This is a fundamental data integrity issue that undermines the entire 'Project Intelligence' module.",
      "prompt": "You are building the real-time Project Health Score engine for SiteSyncAI. This composite score must be trustworthy enough for a CFO to present to their board of directors.

Files to create/modify:
1. Create `src/lib/healthScore.ts` — the scoring engine
2. Modify `src/api/endpoints/projects.ts` — replace hardcoded values with real calculations
3. Modify `src/pages/ProjectHealth.tsx` — show the breakdown of each component score
4. Create `src/components/health/HealthScoreBreakdown.tsx` — visual breakdown component

For `src/lib/healthScore.ts`:
- Export `calculateHealthScore(data: HealthScoreInput): HealthScoreResult`
- HealthScoreInput: { schedulePhases: SchedulePhase[], rfis: RFI[], submittals: Submittal[], budgetItems: BudgetItem[], changeOrders: ChangeOrder[], dailyLogs: DailyLog[], punchItems: PunchItem[], crews: Crew[] }
- HealthScoreResult: { overall: number (0-100), confidence: number (0-100), components: ComponentScore[], trend: 'improving' | 'stable' | 'declining', riskLevel: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'critical' }
- ComponentScore: { name: string, score: number, weight: number, weightedScore: number, details: string, trend: 'up' | 'flat' | 'down', dataPoints: number }

- Calculate these component scores with these weights:
  a) SCHEDULE_PERFORMANCE (weight: 25%):
     - SPI = earned_value / planned_value (from percent_complete vs elapsed time on each phase)
     - Score = clamp(SPI * 100, 0, 100). SPI of 1.0 = score of 100, SPI of 0.8 = score of 80
     - Bonus: +5 if all critical path activities are on or ahead of schedule
     - Penalty: -10 for each critical path activity more than 5 days behind
     - Details: 'Schedule Performance Index: 0.94. 2 of 12 critical path activities behind by an average of 3 days.'

  b) BUDGET_PERFORMANCE (weight: 25%):
     - CPI = earned_value / actual_cost (from budget items: sum of original_amount * percent_complete / sum of actual_amount)
     - Score = clamp(CPI * 100, 0, 100)
     - Penalty: -5 for each CSI division >10% over budget
     - Bonus: +5 if overall project is ≥2% under budget
     - Details: 'Cost Performance Index: 0.97. Division 09 Finishes is 8% over budget ($124K). Overall project tracking 1.2% under budget.'

  c) RFI_HEALTH (weight: 15%):
     - Base score: 100 - (overdue_rfis / total_rfis * 100)
     - Average response time factor: if avg_response_days < 7 then +10, if > 14 then -15
     - Penalty: -5 for each RFI overdue > 14 days
     - Details: 'X of Y RFIs are overdue. Average response time: Z days. 3 RFIs have been open > 30 days.'

  d) SUBMITTAL_HEALTH (weight: 10%):
     - Score based on: (approved_submittals / required_submittals) * 100
     - Penalty: -10 for each submittal where even if approved today, material would arrive late
     - Details: 'X of Y required submittals approved. Z submittals in review. 2 submittals have procurement risk.'

  e) SAFETY_SCORE (weight: 15%):
     - Base score: 100
     - Penalty: -25 for each OSHA recordable incident in last 30 days
     - Penalty: -10 for each near-miss not addressed
     - Bonus: +5 if all toolbox talks completed this month
     - Calculate from daily_logs incident counts and safety observations
     - Details: 'Zero recordable incidents in last 30 days. 12 of 12 toolbox talks completed. RIR: 0.0'

  f) QUALITY_SCORE (weight: 10%):
     - Punch item density: open_punch_items / total_completed_areas
     - First-time pass rate from inspection data
     - Score: 100 - (open_punch_items > threshold ? penalty : 0)
     - Details: 'X punch items open. First-time pass rate: Y%. Punch density: Z items per floor.'

- Confidence calculation: based on data completeness. If daily logs have been submitted every work day = high confidence. If budget has committed costs for >80% of divisions = high. If schedule has percent_complete updated within 7 days = high. Low data = low confidence. Formula: (data_completeness_factors_met / total_factors) * 100

- Trend: compare current overall score with score from 7 days ago (from project_snapshots table). Improving if current > previous + 2, declining if current < previous - 2, stable otherwise.

For `src/api/endpoints/projects.ts`:
- In getMetrics(), replace `aiHealthScore: 85` with a call to calculateHealthScore using the fetched data
- Replace `aiConfidenceLevel: 74` with the real confidence value
- Add `healthComponents` to the return value for the breakdown

For `src/components/health/HealthScoreBreakdown.tsx`:
- Large circular progress ring (120px diameter) showing overall score
  - Ring color: green (>=80), amber (60-79), red (<60)
  - Center: score number (36px bold), 'Project Health' label (12px muted)
- Below ring: trend indicator arrow with text ('Improving', 'Stable', 'Declining')
- Confidence bar: thin horizontal bar showing data confidence
- Component breakdown: 6 rows, each showing:
  - Component name with icon
  - Horizontal progress bar (colored by score)
  - Score number
  - Weight percentage
  - Trend arrow (up/down/flat)
  - Details text (12px, muted, expandable)
- Design: white card, 24px padding, 12px border-radius, follows the 8px grid system
- Responsive: stacks vertically on mobile, side-by-side on desktop

Acceptance criteria:
- Health score changes in real-time when an RFI is closed, a budget item is updated, or a schedule activity progresses
- Each component score has a plain-English explanation a superintendent can understand
- Confidence score accurately reflects data completeness — if no daily logs exist, confidence drops significantly
- Score trend is calculated from historical snapshots, not fabricated
- All numbers are calculated from real Supabase data, zero hardcoded values
- A CFO looking at this number can trust it to represent real project status"
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C1-004",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No AI-powered drawing revision impact analysis — design change cascades are invisible",
      "description": "The product vision calls for 'An AI that monitors drawing revisions and automatically flags which RFIs, submittals, and change orders are affected.' This is a category-defining feature that no competitor has fully implemented. Procore just announced NVIDIA Omniverse integration for digital twins but doesn't do automated revision impact analysis. The current drawings endpoint (src/api/endpoints/documents.ts) has no revision tracking, no linkage to RFIs/submittals, and no impact analysis. Drawing sets (src/pages/Drawings.tsx) don't track versions or flag downstream effects. In construction, a single drawing revision can invalidate 5 submittals, create 3 new RFIs, and trigger change orders. This cascade is currently invisible in SiteSync.",
      "prompt": "You are building the AI Drawing Revision Impact Analysis engine for SiteSyncAI. When a drawing revision is uploaded, the system must automatically identify every RFI, submittal, change order, and punch item that is affected by the change.

Files to create/modify:
1. Create `src/lib/drawingImpactAnalysis.ts` — the core analysis engine
2. Create `src/components/drawings/RevisionImpactPanel.tsx` — visual panel showing affected items
3. Create `src/components/drawings/RevisionTimeline.tsx` — timeline of all revisions with impact counts
4. Modify `src/api/endpoints/documents.ts` — add revision tracking and impact data
5. Create `src/hooks/useDrawingImpact.ts` — hook for consuming impact analysis

For `src/lib/drawingImpactAnalysis.ts`:
- Export `analyzeRevisionImpact(drawing: Drawing, allRFIs: RFI[], allSubmittals: Submittal[], allChangeOrders: ChangeOrder[], allPunchItems: PunchItem[]): RevisionImpact`
- RevisionImpact type: { drawingId: string, sheetNumber: string, revision: string, previousRevision: string, impactedRFIs: ImpactedItem[], impactedSubmittals: ImpactedItem[], impactedChangeOrders: ImpactedItem[], impactedPunchItems: ImpactedItem[], totalImpactedItems: number, riskLevel: 'none' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high', estimatedCostImpact: number, estimatedScheduleImpact: number, recommendations: string[] }
- ImpactedItem type: { id: string, number: string, title: string, impactType: 'may_invalidate' | 'needs_review' | 'superseded' | 'unaffected', reason: string, currentStatus: string }
- Matching logic:
  a) RFI impact: Match RFIs that reference the same drawing sheet_number in their reference_drawing field, or whose description mentions the drawing sheet number. Open RFIs get 'needs_review' impact. Closed RFIs whose response referenced the old revision get 'may_invalidate'.
  b) Submittal impact: Match submittals whose spec_section or description references elements on the changed drawing. If a submittal was 'approved' based on a previous revision, flag as 'needs_review' — the approval may no longer be valid.
  c) Change Order impact: Match COs that originated from RFIs linked to this drawing. If the drawing change resolves an RFI that triggered a CO, flag it.
  d) Punch Item impact: Match punch items whose location or drawing reference matches the changed sheet.
- Generate recommendations: 'Review SUB-003 (Curtain Wall Shop Drawings) — approved against Rev A, now superseded by Rev B. Resubmission may be required.' or 'RFI-012 response referenced detail 4/A301 which has been revised. Verify response is still valid.'

For `src/components/drawings/RevisionImpactPanel.tsx`:
- Slide-in panel from the right (400px wide) when a user views a drawing revision
- Header: sheet number, revision letter, upload date, uploaded by
- Impact summary cards in a row: [RFIs: 3] [Submittals: 2] [COs: 1] [Punch: 0] — colored by severity
- Risk level badge with explanation
- Impacted items list grouped by type:
  - Each item: number (clickable link), title, impact type badge (colored), reason text
  - Impact type badges: red for 'may_invalidate', amber for 'needs_review', green for 'unaffected'
- Recommendations section: numbered action items
- 'Notify Affected Parties' button — generates notification to all users associated with impacted items
- Cost/schedule impact summary at bottom
- Design: follows design standards — white background, 12px border-radius, 8px grid, Lucide icons

For `src/components/drawings/RevisionTimeline.tsx`:
- Vertical timeline showing all revisions for a drawing set
- Each revision node: revision letter, date, impact badge count, who uploaded
- Current revision highlighted with orange dot
- Connecting line between revisions
- Click any revision to see its impact analysis
- Show delta between revisions: '+2 RFIs affected', '-1 CO resolved'

For the modified `src/api/endpoints/documents.ts`:
- Add `getDrawingRevisions(drawingId: string)` — returns all revisions ordered by date
- Add `getRevisionImpact(drawingId: string, revision: string)` — returns computed impact
- Modify `getDrawings()` to include revision_count, latest_revision, and has_impact_items boolean
- Add fields to the drawing map: revisionHistory: Revision[], impactCount: number, latestRevision: string

Acceptance criteria:
- When a new drawing revision is detected, all potentially impacted items are automatically flagged
- Impact analysis runs in <500ms for a project with 200 RFIs, 100 submittals, and 50 COs
- Each impacted item includes a specific, actionable reason for why it's affected
- Recommendations are written in plain construction English that a PM would understand
- The revision timeline shows the complete history of a drawing sheet
- Impact notifications can be sent to all affected parties with one click
